title: Checkpoint Files, Atomic Replacement, Versioning, and Resume · Contract Triage
summary: A preserved Go crawl trace contains one plausible scope, URL, robots, transport, body, tokenizer, goquery, frontier, goroutine, Colly callback, report, security, or shutdown defect; identify the first broken invariant before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in crash-consistent policy-bound checkpoint and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidenc…
objectives: Choose policy, seed, frontier, attempts, digests, records, reports, and counters that must survive versus recomputable state.
Use explicit portable schema, size limits, checksums, compatibility rules, and unknown-field policy rather than implicit struct layout.
Write temporary state, flush and sync under declared durability, validate, rename atomically, sync directory where required, and retain fallback.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and Go Order the regression proof · Debug Offline Resume Audit
steps.instruction:
Order the Offline Resume Audit Authorized site auditing and Go HTTP evidence cycle for this debug task before implementing it.

steps.why: Correct order keeps Offline Resume Audit assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Authorized site auditing and Go Order the regression proof · Debug Offline Resume Audit, keep Offline Resume Audit connected to executable Go authorization, URL, robots, response, tree, frontier, goroutine, report, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.text: A debug Go team handles case 4382 during a keyboard-first accessible report review. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove durable-state boundaries, versioned formats, atomic replacement, in-flight recovery, and offline audit; browser Go remains deterministic and free of native network, parser, filesystem, process, and host effects; then change one tokenizer, goquery, selector, domain constructor, record, link, frontier, or trap case and retain rejected evidence. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser-service, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, x/net/html, goquery, Colly, files, processes, race, load, assistive-technology review, containers, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Load and reconcile checkpoints without network effects, require current authorization, then prove no lost or duplicated state before restart.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and Go Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Offline Resume Audit
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-Go function headed "// Evidence: crawler-go-offline-resume-audit-oi54nz" that uses case 6308 to prove durable-state boundaries, versioned formats, atomic replacement, in-flight recovery, and offline audit. Return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open sockets, resolve DNS, import x/net/html, goquery, Colly, or robotstxt, access host files, retain credentials, launch processes or unbounded goroutines, or claim native parser, race, load, accessibility, privacy, legal, container, restore, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Go 1.26.5, x/net 0.57.0, goquery 1.12.0, Colly 2.3.0, robotstxt 1.1.2, original fixtures, custom RoundTrippers, httptest servers, controlled DNS and browser-service environments, race and fuzz runs, subprocesses, inspected binaries and containers, accessibility review, capacity, backup, restore, rollback, legal review, and release gates.
